global_cues.us_markets: S&P 500 -0.38% to 7,337.11; Nasdaq -0.13% to 25,806.20; Dow -0.63% (-313.62 pts) to 49,596.97. Pullback from record highs as Iran-deal optimism faded; Energy (-1.95%) and Industrials (-1.66%) led declines.
global_cues.european_markets: Mixed prior session — Frankfurt, Paris, Milan firmer earlier in week; FTSE 100 lagging on bank-holiday-shortened week. No major moves before Asia open.
global_cues.asian_markets: Risk-off morning on renewed Iran-US tensions: Nikkei 225 -0.62% (profit-taking after record), KOSPI -1.88%, Hang Seng futures ~26,285 vs prior close 26,626. GIFT Nifty around 24,395 (flat), pointing to a soft-to-flat Indian open.

fii_dii_summary: May 7: FIIs net sellers ₹-340.89 Cr (cash); DIIs net buyers ₹+441.07 Cr — DII support continues to absorb foreign selling. Trend over the week remains FII-light/DII-heavy as global risk-off persists on Middle East worries.
earnings_calendar: SBIN (State Bank of India) - May 8 (Q4 FY26; treasury hit expected to weigh on PAT, focus on margins and slippages)
BANKBARODA (Bank of Baroda) - May 8 (Q4 FY26; dividend consideration; trading window reopens May 11)
TITAN (Titan Company) - May 8 (Q4 FY26; jewellery demand commentary key)
ABB (ABB India) - May 8 (Q4 CY/FY26; capex/order book in focus)
KALYANKJIL (Kalyan Jewellers India) - May 8 (Q4 FY26; jewellery pricing outlook)
OBEROIRLTY (Oberoi Realty) - May 8 (Q4 FY26; Mumbai luxury bookings)
SWIGGY (Swiggy) - May 8 (Q4 FY26; consensus rev ₹4,200-4,600 Cr, PAT loss ₹200-350 Cr, margins -8% to -4%)
HYUNDAI (Hyundai Motor India) - May 8 (Q4 FY26; auto demand commentary)
UJJIVANSFB (Ujjivan Small Finance Bank) - May 8 (Q4 FY26; MFI stress watch)
macro_events: RBI repo rate held at 5.25% (Apr 2026 MPC, neutral stance) — second consecutive pause after Dec-2025 cut; CPI projection FY27 at 4.6% (Q1 4.0%, Q2 4.4%, Q3 5.2%, Q4 4.7%); GDP FY27 at 6.9%.
Strait of Hormuz remains largely blocked since Feb 28, 2026 after US-Israel air war on Iran — ongoing tail risk for crude imports and INR.
India inflation-targeting framework reaffirmed at 4% (±2%) band for Apr-2026 to Mar-2031.
Heavy domestic earnings calendar week: 280+ companies reporting May 4-9; PSU banks (SBIN, BANKBARODA, BANKINDIA) cluster on May 8 — sector-wide read-through expected.
sector_themes: PSU Banks under earnings spotlight: SBIN, BANKBARODA, BANKINDIA report today. Treasury losses risk on rising yields; loan-growth strength is the offset. Nifty PSU Bank -0.49% on May 7 going into the print.
Private Banks resilient: Nifty Private Bank +0.30% on May 7 — relative strength continuing.
Auto strongest sector on May 7 (Nifty Auto +1.93%) — HYUNDAI Q4 today plus prior MARUTI/MM/BAJAJ-AUTO commentary supports demand narrative.
Jewellery cluster reports today: TITAN and KALYANKJIL — gold demand and pricing commentary will set sector tone.
IT weakness: Nifty IT -0.77% on May 7 — weakest sector; INR rebound and US growth jitters weighing on TCS, INFY, WIPRO, HCLTECH.
Pharma flat (+0.10%) — defensive bid as risk-off persists; SUNPHARMA, DRREDDY, CIPLA in focus.
Energy/Oil & Gas: elevated crude (~$97.70 Brent) supports upstream ONGC, OIL but pressures OMCs (BPCL, HPCL, IOC) and aviation (INDIGO).
New-age internet: SWIGGY Q4 today — read-across to ZOMATO, NYKAA, PAYTM on margin trajectory.
Realty: OBEROIRLTY Q4 today — luxury Mumbai bookings indicator for DLF, GODREJPROP, LODHA.
risk_flags: Iran-US conflict re-escalation: Strait of Hormuz blockade ongoing; Asia-Pacific markets opened weak on renewed hostilities reports. Brent crude +2.76% to $97.70/bbl on May 7.
USD/INR at ~94.70 — rebounded from record-low ~95.20 on Iran-deal headlines, but rupee remains structurally vulnerable to any further crude spike. RBI intervention tape to watch.
Crude $100+ risk: any breakdown in US-Iran talks could push Brent back above $100, rekindling India CPI and CAD concerns.
FII outflow persistence: cumulative FII selling pressure continues despite DII support — INR and bond yields exposed if global risk-off deepens.
Earnings event risk: heavy bank-cluster prints on May 8 (SBIN, BANKBARODA, BANKINDIA) — any negative surprise on slippages/margins could drag Nifty Bank and broader Nifty.
IMF warned of global recession risk from prolonged Iran conflict; KOSPI -1.88% and Nikkei -0.62% reflect contagion in Asian sentiment.
brief_summary: Indian markets head into May 8 with a soft-to-flat bias (GIFT Nifty ~24,395) on weak overnight US handoff (S&P -0.38%) and risk-off Asian morning as Iran-US tensions resurface and crude rebounds to ~$97.70. The session is earnings-dominated — SBIN, BANKBARODA, TITAN, TATACONSUM, SWIGGY, HYUNDAI all report today, with PSU bank treasury hits and Swiggy margin trajectory the key swing factors. Continued DII buying is offsetting FII selling, but rupee stability around 94.70 and crude direction remain the dominant macro tail risks.
